title: PPO vs. DPO: When to Use Which
concept: Use PPO when you have complex, multi-step reward signals; use DPO for fast, stable alignment directly from preference pairs.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym DPO stand for in machine learning?
exercises.explain: DPO stands for Direct Preference Optimization, which trains models directly on human preferences without a separate reward model.

cards.title: The Alignment Dilemma
cards.body: Should you teach your AI using a complex reward system, or simply by showing it examples of what you like and dislike? Choosing between PPO and DPO can save you massive amounts of time and computing power!

cards.title: PPO vs. DPO
cards.body: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) updates models using a separate reward system that scores outputs step-by-step. DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) bypasses the extra reward system, tuning the model directly using pairs of preferred (good) and dispreferred (bad) responses.

cards.title: The Training Styles
cards.body: Think of PPO as a dog trainer who gives treats (rewards) for every little trick, step, and movement the dog makes. DPO is like showing a picture of two dogs and simply pointing to the one you prefer. You skip the individual treats and just show the final standard you want.

cards.title: Chatbot Training in Action
cards.body: Imagine training a chatbot. With PPO, you build a second AI helper to rate the chatbot's answers, then use those scores to update the chatbot. With DPO, you simply feed the chatbot pairs of text (e.g., 'Helpful Answer' vs. 'Rude Answer') and tell it to favor the helpful one.

cards.title: Is More Complex Always Better?
cards.body: People often think PPO is always better because it is more complex. In reality, DPO is often preferred because it is faster, uses less memory, and is much simpler to run, while achieving similar or better results for text alignment.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Here is a comparison of how both methods handle model alignment. Choose the one that fits your resources and task structure.

left.text: Requires a separate reward model, uses multi-step feedback, and is great for dynamic environments like games.

right.text: Trains directly on preference pairs, requires no extra reward model, and is fast and stable.

exercises.prompt: What is a major advantage of using DPO over PPO?

options.text: It requires training a separate reward model first.
It is simpler, faster, and more stable to train.
It only works for playing video games.
It uses much more computer memory than PPO.

exercises.explain: DPO is highly stable and fast because it eliminates the need to train and run a separate reward model, updating the policy directly from A/B human preferences.

exercises.prompt: True or False: PPO is generally better suited than DPO for environments with multi-step interactive rewards, like playing a video game.
exercises.explain: PPO is ideal when you have complex, multi-step environments (like games or robotic tasks) where actions get feedback along the way.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the method that does not require a separate reward model.
exercises.sentence: We can train our model faster and save computer memory by using _____ instead of PPO.
exercises.explain: DPO updates the model directly from preference pairs, meaning it doesn't need to run a separate, heavy reward model during training.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO)?

options.text: System A: It predicts the next word in a book.
System B: It learns directly from pairs of 'better' and 'worse' answers.
System C: It uses a separate AI helper to score its outputs.

exercises.explain: System B is DPO because it trains directly on pairs of 'preferred' and 'not preferred' data without a separate reward system. System C is PPO because it uses a separate reward model. System A is just standard pre-training.

exercises.prompt: You are building an AI agent to play a complex strategy game where points are scored dynamically after sequences of moves. Which alignment method should you use and why?

options.text: PPO, because we don't have a reward function.
PPO, because the task requires step-by-step actions and complex environments.
DPO, because step-by-step game scoring is too fast for PPO.
DPO, because it requires setting up a separate reward model.

exercises.explain: PPO is best when you have an active environment (like a game or a complex coding environment) where actions happen step-by-step and need immediate, customized feedback. DPO is ideal when you already have pairs of 'good' vs 'bad' responses and want to train the model directly and quickly.
